titleStmt.title: SII 26.334: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 334. (A.R. No. 316 of 1909.) TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Detached stone built into the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Añjanākshi-ammaṉ shrine in the same temple.

p.hi: Tribhº Tribhuvaṉa-chōḷadēva: Kulōttuṅga III: Year lost. C. 13th century.

summary.p: This inscription is fragmentary. It refers to the king as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam, Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya and to anoint the heroes and the victors’. It records the setting up of an image of the Śaiva saint Tirunāvukkaraśu (i.e., Appar) in the temple of [Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya-nāyaṉār] of Tirukkachchūr alias Nittavinōdanallūr in Śeṅguṉṟa-nāḍu in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍaḷam, by Biramaṉ alias Chittiramē[ḻi ••• •••], son of Marudiḍaṅgoṇḍār, a reciter of the sacred hymns (Tiruppadiyam) in the temple of the deity Biramīśuramuḍaiyanāyanār of a chaturvēdimaṅgalam (name lost) in the same maṇḍalam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
muṉṉāka tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ maturaiyumiḻamum karuvūrum pāṇṭiyaṉ
ṣekamum vicaiya Apiṣekamum paṇṇiyaruḷiṉa tribhuvaṉa coḻatevaṟku yā
maṇṭalattuk kaḷattūr koṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu tirukkaccūrāṉa nittaviṉotanallūr
yanār koyilukku tirunāvukkaracutevarai Eḻuntaruḷuvittāṉ Immaṇṭalattu
jaccatuppetimaṅkalattu Uṭaiyār piramicuramuṭaiya nāyanār koyilil palapa
patiyam viṇṇappañ ceyvār marutiṭaṅkoṇṭār makaṉ piramaṉāṉa cittirame
vittāṉ §
Digital edition of SII 26.334 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
